title: How Agentforce Marketing Can Solve 4 Common Holiday Order Issues
author: Kimberly Pope

sections.heading: Introduction
sections.text: The holidays are a marketer’s dream — hello, increased engagement! — and also the biggest opportunity to build lasting customer relationships. In order to be successful, you need to execute campaigns across every channel that drive sales. But the real challenge is the need to move faster, to personalize even deeper and to connect with customers in more meaningful ways. The good news is AI agents can help you scale your efforts without exhausting your team or losing the human touch.Agentforce Marketingunlocks a new way of working where agents handle the manual tasks and you can focus on what you are best at: strategy and creativity. Here’s a breakdown of four ways agents can solve common holiday marketing headaches.

sections.heading: 1.Scale campaign creation fast
sections.text: The problem:Holiday planning is overwhelming. Marketers spend too much time guessing which messages will resonate and manually segmenting audiences, leading to wasted spend and irrelevant content. The solution:With AI you can accelerate campaign creation, refine audience segments and deploy high-performing content faster than ever before (hours, not weeks). The data generated by your customers’ interactions with your brand is pure gold for marketers. When you use AI to analyze this data, you gain deep, actionable insight into real customer needs and pain points, allowing you to build smarter campaigns and personalize at a depth your competitors can’t match. Best practices and tips: Acceleratecampaign creation.Just describe your campaign goals in natural language. Agentforce instantly turns that into a full campaign brief. It then auto-generates ready-to-edit drafts of emails, SMS messages and even customer journeys, giving your team a huge head start.Personalize based on segmentation.Forget complicated SQL queries. When data is unified, you can define your audience segments using simple prompts instead. This makes targeting faster and more accessible to every marketer. You can then target these precise segments with ultra-personalized loyalty campaigns or upsell opportunities to drive powerful, meaningful connections.Intelligently determine the next step.Replace static campaigns with dynamic, intelligent routing. Agentforce powersJourney Decisioning, automatically assigning each customer to their ideal next step based on real-time data. This means no more guessing which path fits best — each journey adapts based on who the customer is, what they’ve done and what they need in that moment.

sections.heading: 2.Reminders to complete a purchase
sections.text: The problem:A customer fills their cart with gifts, gets distracted and leaves before checking out. That’s lost revenue, and it happens more often during the busy holidays. The solution:You can win back that revenue by using real-time targeting to create a highly personalized, sequenced recovery campaign across multiple channels. This isn’t just about sending a generic “Did you forget something?” message. It’s about using your data to understand the shopper’s intent and sending a perfectly timed message on the channel they check most, with the ability to offer instant, frictionless support if they have a final question. Best practices and tips: Use insights for decision-making.Analyze pastmarketing performanceand pull in context across all of your data with the help of Agentforce. Use these insights to optimize your incentives and frequency of engagement.Trigger a multi-channel journey.Automatically deploy a staggered series of alerts, as soon as the cart is abandoned. Start with an email within an hour of abandonment for instant recall, then follow up in another channel if no action has been taken.Personalize the nudge.Don’t send a generic reminder. Pull the customer’s name and the specific item name into the push notification or text message, making the message feel personal and driving a much higher conversion rate.

sections.heading: 3.Streamline transactional alerts
sections.text: The problem:“Where’s my order?” is the most frequent question during the holiday rush. Order delays and logistical issues lead to frustrated customers and an overwhelmed support team. The solution:Proactively send real-time order tracking updates to keep customers informed across their preferred channels, dramatically reducing inbound service requests. You don’t want your customers’ first interaction with your service team to be an angry call asking where their package is. By using automated alerts, you eliminate the friction and turn a necessary transactional message into a positive engagement opportunity, backed by instant help if a real problem arises. Best practices and tips: Automate transactional alerts.Trigger real-time updates for order confirmations, when items ship, and at delivery. This manages expectations before the customer even thinks to ask.Adapt your strategy.Recent changes fromiOS and Androidare reshaping how subscribers see, engage with and prioritize messages and notifications. Make sure you understand how this impacts high-priority, time-sensitive alerts like this.Provide instant help.Invite customers to reply to your promotional messages if they need help.Two-waySMS and WhatsApp connect the customer to an AI agent that can immediately answer questions. Two-way email will be generally available in February 2026.

sections.heading: 4.Enhance post-purchase service
sections.text: The Problem:Holiday shoppers expect instant, reliable support. Dealing with general order follow-up and the influx of post-holiday returns can create a disjointed experience and damage long-term loyalty. The Solution:Create a unified serviceframeworkwhere the entire post-purchase journey, including complex returns and general inquiries, is handled seamlessly and intelligently across all channels. When a customer reaches out for help, they expect speed and they hate repeating themselves. By creating a frictionless path for returns and offering proactive, informed service, you turn a necessary operational process into a powerful loyalty driver. Connected shopping experience.If a customer needs help, AI agents can autonomouslyassist customersto resolve general inquiries. Agentforce can pull the order history, confirm the return window, and provide tracking updates — all without human intervention.Seamless human hand-off.The AI agent can automatically escalate to a human, when needed. The human agent receives the full transcript and customer history, eliminating the need for the customer to repeat themselves.Provide added value.Follow up with customers after the purchase. Send a targeted campaign with a complimentary offer, next best action, or details on how to use the product. This reinforces trust, while increasing customer satisfaction.

sections.heading: A unified platform for a unified experience
sections.text: The holidays don’t have to be a stressful balancing act. By using a single, unified platform, you can create a seamless, omnichannel experience that puts customer satisfaction first.Agentforce Marketing’s automation and personalization capabilities gives you the scalability to handle the busiest seasons with confidence. Ultimately, it’s about using customer data to deliver tailored messages that resonate and offer quick, reliable support. This approach not only boosts sales and reduces churn but also builds a foundation of trust that keeps customers coming back, long after the decorations are put away.

raw_html:
The holidays are a marketer’s dream — hello, increased engagement! — and also the biggest opportunity to build lasting customer relationships. In order to be successful, you need to execute campaigns across every channel that drive sales. But the real challenge is the need to move faster, to personalize even deeper and to connect with customers in more meaningful ways.

The good news is AI agents can help you scale your efforts without exhausting your team or losing the human touch. Agentforce Marketing unlocks a new way of working where agents handle the manual tasks and you can focus on what you are best at: strategy and creativity. Here’s a breakdown of four ways agents can solve common holiday marketing headaches.

1. Scale campaign creation fast

The problem: Holiday planning is overwhelming. Marketers spend too much time guessing which messages will resonate and manually segmenting audiences, leading to wasted spend and irrelevant content.

The solution: With AI you can accelerate campaign creation, refine audience segments and deploy high-performing content faster than ever before (hours, not weeks).

The data generated by your customers’ interactions with your brand is pure gold for marketers. When you use AI to analyze this data, you gain deep, actionable insight into real customer needs and pain points, allowing you to build smarter campaigns and personalize at a depth your competitors can’t match.

Best practices and tips:

  • Accelerate campaign creation. Just describe your campaign goals in natural language. Agentforce instantly turns that into a full campaign brief. It then auto-generates ready-to-edit drafts of emails, SMS messages and even customer journeys, giving your team a huge head start.
  • Personalize based on segmentation. Forget complicated SQL queries. When data is unified, you can define your audience segments using simple prompts instead. This makes targeting faster and more accessible to every marketer. You can then target these precise segments with ultra-personalized loyalty campaigns or upsell opportunities to drive powerful, meaningful connections.
  • Intelligently determine the next step. Replace static campaigns with dynamic, intelligent routing. Agentforce powers Journey Decisioning, automatically assigning each customer to their ideal next step based on real-time data. This means no more guessing which path fits best — each journey adapts based on who the customer is, what they’ve done and what they need in that moment.

New Rules of Engagement: A Marketer’s Guide to iOS26 and Android 16 Changes

4 Tips to Help Get Your Email Campaigns Holiday-Ready

2. Reminders to complete a purchase

The problem: A customer fills their cart with gifts, gets distracted and leaves before checking out. That’s lost revenue, and it happens more often during the busy holidays.

The solution: You can win back that revenue by using real-time targeting to create a highly personalized, sequenced recovery campaign across multiple channels.

This isn’t just about sending a generic “Did you forget something?” message. It’s about using your data to understand the shopper’s intent and sending a perfectly timed message on the channel they check most, with the ability to offer instant, frictionless support if they have a final question.

Best practices and tips:

  • Use insights for decision-making. Analyze past marketing performance and pull in context across all of your data with the help of Agentforce. Use these insights to optimize your incentives and frequency of engagement.
  • Trigger a multi-channel journey. Automatically deploy a staggered series of alerts, as soon as the cart is abandoned. Start with an email within an hour of abandonment for instant recall, then follow up in another channel if no action has been taken.
  • Personalize the nudge. Don’t send a generic reminder. Pull the customer’s name and the specific item name into the push notification or text message, making the message feel personal and driving a much higher conversion rate.

3. Streamline transactional alerts

The problem: “Where’s my order?” is the most frequent question during the holiday rush. Order delays and logistical issues lead to frustrated customers and an overwhelmed support team.

The solution: Proactively send real-time order tracking updates to keep customers informed across their preferred channels, dramatically reducing inbound service requests.

You don’t want your customers’ first interaction with your service team to be an angry call asking where their package is. By using automated alerts, you eliminate the friction and turn a necessary transactional message into a positive engagement opportunity, backed by instant help if a real problem arises.

Best practices and tips:

  • Automate transactional alerts. Trigger real-time updates for order confirmations, when items ship, and at delivery. This manages expectations before the customer even thinks to ask.
  • Adapt your strategy. Recent changes from iOS and Android are reshaping how subscribers see, engage with and prioritize messages and notifications. Make sure you understand how this impacts high-priority, time-sensitive alerts like this.
  • Provide instant help. Invite customers to reply to your promotional messages if they need help. Two-way SMS and WhatsApp connect the customer to an AI agent that can immediately answer questions. Two-way email will be generally available in February 2026.

4. Enhance post-purchase service

The Problem: Holiday shoppers expect instant, reliable support. Dealing with general order follow-up and the influx of post-holiday returns can create a disjointed experience and damage long-term loyalty.

The Solution: Create a unified service framework where the entire post-purchase journey, including complex returns and general inquiries, is handled seamlessly and intelligently across all channels.

When a customer reaches out for help, they expect speed and they hate repeating themselves. By creating a frictionless path for returns and offering proactive, informed service, you turn a necessary operational process into a powerful loyalty driver.

  • Connected shopping experience. If a customer needs help, AI agents can autonomously assist customers to resolve general inquiries. Agentforce can pull the order history, confirm the return window, and provide tracking updates — all without human intervention.
  • Seamless human hand-off. The AI agent can automatically escalate to a human, when needed. The human agent receives the full transcript and customer history, eliminating the need for the customer to repeat themselves. 
  • Provide added value. Follow up with customers after the purchase. Send a targeted campaign with a complimentary offer, next best action, or details on how to use the product. This reinforces trust, while increasing customer satisfaction. 

A unified platform for a unified experience

The holidays don’t have to be a stressful balancing act. By using a single, unified platform, you can create a seamless, omnichannel experience that puts customer satisfaction first. Agentforce Marketing’s automation and personalization capabilities gives you the scalability to handle the busiest seasons with confidence.

Ultimately, it’s about using customer data to deliver tailored messages that resonate and offer quick, reliable support. This approach not only boosts sales and reduces churn but also builds a foundation of trust that keeps customers coming back, long after the decorations are put away.
